Operation 'submitPaymentForm' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. Original summary is generic. Description lacks clarity about the return value and the critical side effect of redirecting to eSewa's payment gateway, which is essential for AI agents to understand the operation's full workflow. Added domain-specific terminology (HMAC-SHA256 signature, eSewa wallet) and clarified the response structure and user redirection behavior.

data.current_description: Initiates a payment transaction by submitting a payment form with merchant details, amounts, and signature. All parameters are required and must not be null or empty.
data.description_suggestion: Initiates a payment transaction by submitting a payment form containing merchant details, transaction amount, and HMAC-SHA256 signature. All parameters are required and must not be null or empty. Returns a payment response containing transaction confirmation details. This operation redirects the user to eSewa's payment gateway for wallet authentication and payment completion.

message: Operation 'getTransactionStatus' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, missing_input_details, missing_output_details, poor_ai_interpretability. Original summary is too generic. Description mentions endpoints but lacks critical context about when to use this operation (post-payment verification), what parameters are required, authentication requirements, and what the response contains. Enhanced with domain context about transaction verification workflow, signature authentication, and clear guidance on endpoint selection for AI agents to properly determine operation usage.

data.current_description: Retrieves the current status of a transaction using the product code, transaction UUID, and total amount. Available on production server at https://esewa.com.np/api/epay/transaction/status/ or testing at https://rc.esewa.com.np/api/epay/transaction/status/
data.description_suggestion: Verifies the status of a completed eSewa payment transaction by querying with product code, transaction UUID, and total amount as required parameters. This operation should be called after payment completion to confirm transaction success and retrieve transaction details. Returns a transaction status response containing payment confirmation status, transaction reference, and merchant information. Requires HMAC-SHA256 signature authentication. Use production endpoint (https://esewa.com.np/api/epay/transaction/status/) for live transactions or testing endpoint (https://rc.esewa.com.np/api/epay/transaction/status/) for sandbox testing.
data.reasoning: Original summary is too generic. Description mentions endpoints but lacks critical context about when to use this operation (post-payment verification), what parameters are required, authentication requirements, and what the response contains. Enhanced with domain context about transaction verification workflow, signature authentication, and clear guidance on endpoint selection for AI agents to properly determine operation usage.

message: Operation 'getTransactionStatus' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, missing_input_details, missing_output_details, poor_ai_interpretability. Original summary is too generic and doesn't convey the verification/confirmation purpose. Description mentions parameters but doesn't explain their purpose or what response fields are returned, making it difficult for AI agents to understand when to use this operation and how to interpret results. data.current_description: Retrieves the current status of a transaction using the product code, transaction UUID, and total amount. Available on production server at https://esewa.com.np/api/epay/transaction/status/ or testing at https://rc.esewa.com.np/api/epay/transaction/status/
data.description_suggestion: Verifies the status of a completed eSewa payment transaction by querying with product code, transaction UUID, and total amount as required parameters. Returns transaction status details including payment confirmation status, transaction reference, and merchant information. This operation is used post-payment to confirm whether the customer's payment was successfully processed. Available on production (https://esewa.com.np/api/epay/transaction/status/) and testing (https://rc.esewa.com.np/api/epay/transaction/status/) environments.
